findings.title: Library docstring still uses old product name 'Nightwatch'
findings.description: The package docstring at the top of noxaudit/__init__.py reads: "Nightwatch: Nightly AI-powered codebase audits." The project has been rebranded to Noxaudit across other files and docs, so this is stale and may confuse users or automated tooling that extracts package metadata.
findings.suggestion: Update the module docstring to reference 'Noxaudit' (or keep it generic). Example: "Noxaudit: Nightly AI-powered codebase audits."

findings.title: Report header uses old product name 'Nightwatch'
findings.description: generate_report() emits a markdown heading '# Nightwatch Report: ...' which does not match the project's current name (Noxaudit). This causes inconsistent user-facing output and diverging branding across reports and documentation.
findings.suggestion: Change the generated report title to reference Noxaudit (or derive from package metadata). Example: '# Noxaudit Report: {_focus_display(result.focus)}'.

findings.title: Action installs repository code from the default branch without pinning (supply-chain risk)
findings.description: The composite Action installs the package with `pip install git+https://github.com/atriumn/noxaudit.git` (no ref or pinned commit). Installing unpinned code from a VCS URL in CI exposes workflows to supply-chain risks: a malicious commit or branch takeover could alter the installed code used in your actions.
findings.suggestion: Pin the installer to a specific commit SHA / tag (for example `git+https://github.com/atriumn/noxaudit.git@v0.1.0`) or publish and use a fixed package version from PyPI. Prefer pinned, audited artifacts in CI actions.

findings.title: GitHub token environment variable name mismatch
findings.description: The action sets GH_TOKEN in the env block (`GH_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.github-token }}`) but project documentation and some integration instructions reference GITHUB_TOKEN (the standard secret name provided by GitHub Actions). Other code/docs (docs/integrations/github-issues.md) instruct using GITHUB_TOKEN. This mismatch may break workflows that rely on the standard GITHUB_TOKEN env var.
findings.suggestion: Use the conventional GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable name (set `GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.github-token }}`), or clearly document and wire the alternate name consistently across docs and code. Ensure the code paths that expect GITHUB_TOKEN are satisfied by the action environment.

findings.title: Pre-pass config field name differs from docs (auto vs auto_disable)
findings.description: The dataclass PrepassConfig exposes `auto_disable: bool = False` while documentation and examples use `prepass.auto` (true/false). load_config() maps the YAML `auto` key into `auto_disable = not prepass_raw.get('auto', True)`. This inversion and naming mismatch is confusing and non-intuitive for users editing their config.
findings.suggestion: Either (A) change PrepassConfig to expose `auto: bool = True` and use that name throughout code and docs, or (B) update all docs and examples to use `prepass.auto_disable` and explain the semantics. Keep naming consistent and avoid boolean inversion surprises.

findings.title: Duplicate / inconsistent dev dependency declarations
findings.description: pyproject.toml contains dev/test dependencies in two places: [project.optional-dependencies].dev and [dependency-groups].dev with different version constraints (pytest>=8.0 vs pytest>=9.0.2, ruff versions differ). This duplication can lead to confusion or inconsistent environments for contributors and CI.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate dev dependencies to a single location (prefer the PEP-621 `optional-dependencies` or the dependency-groups used by your build tooling) and keep version pins consistent. Document which section is authoritative for contributors and CI.

findings.title: Script uses .git/hooks symlink without checking for git repo
findings.description: scripts/setup-hooks.sh symlinks the pre-push hook into the repo's .git/hooks directory without checking whether the current checkout is actually a git repo. Running this script outside a git worktree may produce confusing errors.
findings.suggestion: Add a guard that checks for .git directory existence and aborts with a helpful message if missing. Example: if [ ! -d "$REPO_ROOT/.git" ]; then echo 'Not a git repo'; exit 1; fi

findings.title: Fallback hardcoded version mismatches package version
findings.description: _get_noxaudit_version() returns the package __version__ when available, but falls back to a hardcoded '0.2.0'. The package __version__ (noxaudit/__init__.py) is '0.1.0'. The fallback value differs from the declared package version and may cause inconsistent tool metadata in SARIF output.
findings.suggestion: Avoid hardcoding a fallback that diverges from package versioning; prefer a safe fallback like 'unknown' or derive the version consistently. Keep package version and fallback aligned, or raise a clear warning if not available.

findings.title: README references placeholder API keys that look like real secrets
findings.description: The README shows examples like `export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...` and `export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...`. While these are placeholders, they resemble real key formats and may encourage accidental pasting of real keys into sample config files or examples committed to repos.
findings.suggestion: Use clearly fake placeholders that cannot be mistaken for real keys (e.g. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='ANTHROPIC_TEST_KEY') and add an explicit note reminding users not to commit real keys.

findings.title: Action cache keys and paths use placeholder or constant keys that may not restore correctly
findings.description: The composite action uses actions/cache/restore and save steps with fixed keys like `noxaudit-pending-will-not-match` and `noxaudit-retrieved-will-not-match`. These keys are unlikely to collide with previous runs and the `restore-keys` are generic; this reduces the usefulness of caching pending state between workflows.
findings.suggestion: Use a deterministic key based on repository + workflow inputs (or use the run id only for save and the same key for restore when appropriate). If cross-run persistence is required, document the intended caching semantics. Avoid placeholder keys that imply 'will-not-match'.

findings.title: Action runs `pip install` from an unverified remote without offline reproducibility
findings.description: The Action installs package code from the upstream repository at runtime (`pip install git+https://...`). Running unverified installs at CI time increases supply chain risk and can lead to non-reproducible builds if the upstream repository changes.
findings.suggestion: Pin to a tag or commit hash and/or use a released package artifact (PyPI) with a pinned version. Add signature verification or checksum validation for critical artifacts.

findings.title: Docs show `provider_rotation` default and examples inconsistent with config dataclass defaults
findings.description: Documentation and config examples show a variety of defaults for provider/model (some show `gemini`, others `anthropic`, and model strings vary between 'claude-sonnet-4-5' and 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929'). Inconsistent examples make it unclear what the effective default behavior is.
findings.suggestion: Audit documentation to present a single authoritative default and make explicit which model string is the canonical default. Prefer deriving displayed defaults dynamically from code where possible to avoid drift.

findings.title: Auto pre-pass economics logic uses config.model rather than per-repo provider/model
findings.description: _maybe_prepass() looks up `model_key = config.model` to decide whether tier_threshold is exceeded and to auto-enable pre-pass. However, repositories can override provider/model per-repo using provider_rotation and providers config; using the global config.model may produce incorrect pre-pass decisions for repos that specify a different model/provider.
findings.suggestion: Resolve the effective model for the target repo/provider (using config.get_model_for_provider or similar) before applying tier_threshold logic, so pre-pass activation reflects the actual model that will be used for that repo.

findings.title: Decision file hashing uses 16-character truncated SHA; collision risk for stable ids
findings.description: create_baseline_decisions and _hash_file compute file_hash using hashlib.sha256(...).hexdigest()[:16]. Truncating to 16 hex characters (64 bits) introduces a small collision risk which could cause resurface logic to behave incorrectly when different files/contents produce the same truncated hash.
findings.suggestion: Store the full hex digest or a longer prefix (e.g., 32 hex chars / 128 bits) to make accidental collisions negligible. If disk size is a concern, document the tradeoff and consider storing the full digest in compressed form.

findings.title: Response parsing assumes Anthropic message.content[0].text exists
findings.description: _parse_response() accesses message.content[0].text directly. If the Anthropic SDK message shape changes or the response contains multiple content parts, this direct indexing could raise an IndexError or miss other content.
findings.suggestion: Add defensive code to iterate parts, check for presence, and handle alternate response shapes robustly. Log or raise descriptive errors when expected fields are missing.

findings.title: OpenAI provider uses JSON-lines with `method`/`url` and expects specific batch API fields
findings.description: submit_batch() builds a JSONL request that relies on a particular OpenAI batch API shape; if the installed openai version or API behavior differs, batch submission may fail in subtle ways (and the cli has limited validation).
findings.suggestion: Validate constructed JSONL strictly (and add unit tests that assert the exact structure). Consider documenting minimal supported openai client versions; guard against missing attributes at runtime with clear error messages.

findings.title: Contributor instructions reference `uv pip install -e "\.[dev]"` and mixed `uv` usage
findings.description: Several docs show `uv pip install -e "[dev]"` and `uv run pytest`. `uv` is a lesser-known tool; mixing `uv` and direct `pip`/`pytest` commands may confuse contributors. There are also variations between CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and docs pages.
findings.suggestion: Standardize contributor instructions to show both the `uv`-based commands and a plain `pip`/`python -m` alternative (e.g., `pip install -e '.[dev]'`) so contributors without `uv` can follow. Keep docs consistent across files.

findings.title: Telegram notifications may leak Markdown content or be impacted by unescaped user-provided text
findings.description: send_telegram() sends `message` with parse_mode set to 'Markdown' directly. If message content includes user-provided strings (finding titles, file paths) with Markdown control characters, the message layout can be altered or render incorrectly. This is a minor information-delivery risk but can lead to misleading notifications.
findings.suggestion: Escape user-controlled strings before embedding them into Markdown (or use 'MarkdownV2' with careful escaping, or send plain text). Make sure message construction in reporter.format_notification safely handles arbitrary file/finding text.

findings.title: Roadmap doc references removed OSS features and other internal product terms
findings.description: This doc mentions features 'moved to SaaS' and references internal concepts (frames, SaaS scheduling). Presenting these as removed may confuse OSS users; it's acceptable for roadmap but should be clearly labeled as 'SaaS-only'.
findings.suggestion: Add a clear banner indicating 'SaaS-only' and remove or link to OSS alternatives so OSS users understand which features remain in CLI vs SaaS.

findings.title: project.scripts entrypoint declared but CLI packaging may conflict with [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages setting
findings.description: pyproject.toml declares [project.scripts] noxaudit=noxaudit.cli:main and also defines hatch wheel packaging 'packages = ["noxaudit"]'. This is normal, but verify packaging settings match the intended published package layout. Inconsistent packaging settings across build configs sometimes cause missing files in wheels.
findings.suggestion: Confirm packaging config by building a wheel locally and testing the installed CLI entrypoint. Ensure MANIFEST/pyproject include non-code resources (docs/assets) you need.
